rules:
Each player reveals the top 3 cards of their deck and puts all Pokémon they find there into their hand. Then, each player shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, put it onto your Bench, and shuffle your deck. If tails, put this Egg Incubator on the bottom of your deck instead of in the discard pile.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.effect: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.effect: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Professor Willow

rules:
Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Draconic Star
attacks.effect: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and attach any number of Water or Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Juggernaut
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each extra Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 120 damage in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.effect: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.effect: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Professor Willow

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Arm Charge
attacks.effect: You may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Growing Tall
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Head Swing
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Once during each player's turn, that player may discard 3 cards from the top of their deck. If a player discarded any Item cards in this way, they put those Item cards into their hand.

Each player reveals the top 3 cards of their deck and puts all Pokémon they find there into their hand. Then, each player shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, put it onto your Bench, and shuffle your deck. If tails, put this Egg Incubator on the bottom of your deck instead of in the discard pile.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you're scattering food for them, be careful— several hundred of them can gather at once.
It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Tilt Fling
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that's proof it really likes you.

attacks.name: Bustle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

attacks.name: Whiny Voice
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.
It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

attacks.name: Enriching Egg
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Whenever a Blissey finds a weakened Pokémon, it will share its egg and offer its care until the other Pokémon is all better.

attacks.name: Delicious Egg
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The egg Chansey carries is not only delicious but also packed with nutrition. It's used as a high-class cooking ingredient.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Draconic Star
attacks.effect: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and attach any number of Water or Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Juggernaut
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each extra Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 120 damage in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Arm Charge
attacks.effect: You may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swinging Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Iron Buster
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

attacks.name: Raging Crash
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body can't be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.

attacks.name: Chase Up
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.effect: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: It commands a nest of Rattata. Different nests don't get along, whipping up severe fights over feeding grounds.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Night after night, they sneak into people's homes seeking food. A massive outbreak of them has become an issue of public concern.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

attacks.name: Crashing Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

attacks.name: Rock Tomb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Raging Swing
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

attacks.name: Souvenir
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wonder Flash
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: By releasing enmity-erasing waves from its ribbonlike feelers, Sylveon stops any conflict.

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

attacks.name: Pinpoint Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.effect: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.effect: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

attacks.name: Buddy Bolt
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.
It's nature's cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.
It's said that this Pokémon's beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Wreak Havoc
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Wild Splash
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

attacks.name: Lively Grouping
attacks.effect: Search your deck for any number of Magikarp, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Raging Fin
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Magikarp and Gyarados in your discard pile.

flavorText: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

attacks.name: Tumbling Tackle
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

attacks.name: Twilight Inspiration
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining. Take 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Slowpoke became Slowbro when a Shellder bit on to its tail. Sweet flavors seeping from the tail make the Shellder feel as if its life is a dream.

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ideal Fishing Day
attacks.effect: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Slow-witted and oblivious, this Pokémon won't feel any pain if its tail gets eaten. It won't notice when its tail grows back, either.

attacks.name: Torrential Cannon
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Torrential Cannon.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.
When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If angered, the humps on its back erupt in a shower of molten lava. It lives in the craters of volcanoes.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.
It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.effect: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Tail on Fire
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Poison String-Up
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

attacks.name: Growing Tall
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Head Swing
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pollen Hazard
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.
Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.name: Summoning Aroma
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.
There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.
